data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness strictly beats SC iff base error e is in the majority-fragile zone 1/(k+1) < e < 1/2 AND critic precision P > 1/2 AND recall R > 0; harness loses (strictly underperforms SC) when P < 1/2 because false flags flip more correct majorities than true flags fix broken ones.", "mechanism": "SC majority is wrong iff at least ceil(k/2) candidates err. In the fragile zone this probability is nonzero but below 1/2, so SC is sometimes wrong. Harness fixes a broken majority only when the critic flags enough error candidates to prevent the wrong answer from surviving (needs R high enough to catch >1 error in a broken batch). Harness breaks a correct majority when the critic false-flags enough correct candidates to flip the plurality to a wrong answer; this happens with probability proportional to (1-P)/e. Net gain = P_fix * P(SC wrong) - P_break * P(SC right). When P < 1/2 the false-flag rate exceeds the true-flag rate, so P_break dominates and harness nets negative.", "scope": {"covers": ["k=5, e in (0.17, 0.50), P in (0.5, 1.0), R in (0, 1]", "k=3, e in (0.25, 0.50), P in (0.5, 1.0)", "k=7, e in (0.125, 0.50), P in (0.5, 1.0)", "counterexample regime P < 0.5 where harness strictly loses to SC"], "excludes": ["e < 1/(k+1): SC already near ceiling, no room to improve", "e >= 1/2: SC already failing majority, harness cannot reliably recover", "question-conditional critic calibration that varies P per item"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "At k=5, e=0.30, P=0.7, R=0.8: harness accuracy equals SC accuracy (tie, no strict gain) despite being inside the predicted winning zone", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At k=5, e=0.30, P=0.3, R=0.9: harness accuracy strictly exceeds SC (gain despite P < 1/2, contradicting the false-flag loss prediction)", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At k=5, e=0.05, P=0.9, R=0.9: harness strictly beats SC (gain outside the fragile zone, contradicting the necessary-condition claim)", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At k=5, e=0.45, P=0.9, R=0.2: harness strictly beats SC by more than 5 points (gain with recall too low to catch enough errors in a broken majority)", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "This is the fixed-regime generalization of the observed data. The v4-flash easy result (e=0.033, P=0.333) falls outside the fragile zone AND has P < 1/2, so the prediction is no gain — matching the tie. The v4-pro hard result (e=0.01, P=0.125) is deep in the ceiling regime, so no gain — matching. The decisive experiment targets the fragile zone with controlled P above and below 1/2. Budget: 60 items * (5 candidates + 5 critiques + 5 re-votes) * ~600 tokens/call = ~180k tokens, plus answer-key evaluation ~20k, well within 300k."}
